question: Welcome to your first week! What is the primary purpose of a business?

answers.description: The primary purpose of a business is to provide goods and services to customers.
While creating job opportunities is important, it is not the primary purpose of a business.
Reducing environmental impact is a goal for many businesses, but not the primary purpose.
Engaging in charity work is commendable, but it is not the primary purpose of a business.

outcome: understanding of external factors influencing a small business

question: What is the term for the amount by which revenue exceeds costs?

answers.description: Profit is the amount by which revenue exceeds costs.
Loss occurs when costs exceed revenue.
Revenue is the total income from sales.
Expense refers to the costs incurred by a business.

outcome: understanding of price determination for an organisation

question: With your experience setting prices, what impact does the price have on the demand?

answers.description: Increasing price decreases demand as products are deemed not worth it.
Decreasing price typically increases demand as products are deemed a good deal.
As price decreases, demand will increase as customers view this as a good deal.

answers.text: The price is not relevant to the demand
answers.description: Price is always related to demand.

outcome: understanding of how interdependent financial relations are affected by an event

question: With your experience setting staff numbers, which of these is not a true statement regarding workers?

answers.text: More workers results in increased profit
answers.description: Without sufficient demand, more workers can result in lost profit.

answers.text: Less workers results in increased profit
answers.description: While money upfront can be saved, supply can struggle without sufficient staff.

answers.text: More workers results in increased supply and demand
answers.description: Supply is increased, but demand is an external factor.

answers.text: More workers results in increased supply
answers.description: Workers directly correspond to the supply of a businesss.

outcome: understanding of the financial viability of an organization
